title: pteridium latiusculum subsp. latiusculum
intro: Pteridium latiusculum subsp. latiusculum, also known as bracken fern, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family, primarily found in the southern hemisphere, including Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Pteridium latiusculum subsp. latiusculum include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area for therapeutic purposes.
